Here is the complete, verified catalogue of every Go source file in **kubernetes/kubernetes v1.32.0** that implements API-server audit logging, organised by the four functional areas you asked about.

---

## 1. Audit Event Creation from Requests

These files create and populate `audit.Event` objects from incoming HTTP requests across all three lifecycle stages.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/context.go` | `AuditContext` struct (holds the in-flight event + `RequestAuditConfig`); `WithAuditContext` / `AuditContextFrom` (store/retrieve from `context.Context`); `WithAuditID`; thread-safe `AddAuditAnnotation` / `AddAuditAnnotations` / `AddAuditAnnotationsMap`; `AuditEventFrom`; `GetAuditIDTruncated` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/request.go` | **Core population functions**: `LogRequestMetadata` (verb, URI, user, source IPs, object ref), `LogImpersonatedUser`, `LogRequestObject` (serialises request body → `event.RequestObject`, optionally strips managed fields), `LogRequestPatch`, `LogResponseObject` (serialises response body → `event.ResponseObject`); helpers `encodeObject`, `copyWithoutManagedFields`, `maybeTruncateUserAgent` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/audit_init.go` | `WithAuditInit` middleware: allocates `AuditContext` on the request context, assigns `Audit-ID` (from header or fresh UUID), echoes it in the response header |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/audit.go` | `WithAudit` middleware: calls `evaluatePolicyAndCreateAuditEvent`, drives the three-stage lifecycle (`StageRequestReceived` → `StageResponseStarted` for long-running → `StageResponseComplete` / `StagePanic`); `processAuditEvent` (sets `StageTimestamp`, writes latency annotations, calls `sink.ProcessEvents`); `auditResponseWriter` (intercepts `WriteHeader`/`Write`/`Hijack` to capture HTTP status and emit `StageResponseStarted` for streaming); `writeLatencyToAnnotation` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authn_audit.go` | `WithFailedAuthenticationAudit`: wraps the 401 handler to emit a `StageResponseStarted` event for failed-authentication requests, recording which credential types were attempted |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/audit.go` | `WithAudit` admission decorator: after each `Admit`/`Validate` call, flushes admission-plugin annotations into the audit event via `AddAuditAnnotationsMap` |

---

## 2. Audit Policy Evaluation

These files define the policy data model, load it from disk, and evaluate which requests to audit at which level.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/evaluator.go` | `PolicyRuleEvaluator` interface (`EvaluatePolicyRule`); `RequestAuditConfig` struct (Level, OmitStages, OmitManagedFields) — the result of evaluating policy against one request |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/policy/checker.go` | `NewPolicyRuleEvaluator` / `policyRuleEvaluator.EvaluatePolicyRule`: iterates rules in order, calls `ruleMatches` → `ruleMatchesResource` / `ruleMatchesNonResource` (wildcard, subresource, resource-name matching), returns first match or policy-level defaults; `NewFakePolicyRuleEvaluator` for tests |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/policy/reader.go` | `LoadPolicyFromFile` / `LoadPolicyFromBytes`: reads YAML/JSON policy file, decodes via audit scheme, validates, returns internal `Policy` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/policy/util.go` | `AllStages`, `AllLevels`, `InvertStages`, `ConvertStagesToStrings`, `ConvertStringSetToStages` — stage-set arithmetic used by policy processing |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit/types.go` | Internal audit API types: `Level` constants (`None`/`Metadata`/`Request`/`RequestResponse`), `Stage` constants, `Event`, `Policy`, `PolicyRule`, `ObjectReference`, `GroupResources` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit/helpers.go` | `Level.Less` / `Level.GreaterOrEqual` — ordering used in policy evaluation and event-body gating |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit/validation/validation.go` | `ValidatePolicy` / `validatePolicyRule`: validates legal Level/Stage values, no conflicting resource+nonResourceURL fields; called by `LoadPolicyFromBytes` |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit/v1/types.go` | External `audit.k8s.io/v1` types (wire format for policy files and webhook payloads) |

---

## 3. Audit Backend Dispatch (log file vs. webhook)

These files define the `Backend`/`Sink` interfaces, implement the two concrete backends, provide the buffering layer, and wire everything together.

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/types.go` | `Sink` interface (`ProcessEvents`); `Backend` interface (`Sink` + `Run` + `Shutdown` + `String`) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/union.go` | `Union` backend: fans out `ProcessEvents`/`Run`/`Shutdown` to multiple delegate backends simultaneously (used when both log and webhook are configured) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/audit/log/backend.go` | **Log-file backend**: `NewBackend` (writes to `io.Writer`); `logEvent` serialises each event as legacy single-line text (`FormatLegacy` via `EventString`) or structured JSON (`FormatJson` via audit codec) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/audit/webhook/webhook.go` | **Webhook backend**: `NewBackend` (loads kubeconfig, creates `GenericWebhook` REST client); `NewDynamicBackend` (pre-built REST client); `processEvents` batches events into `EventList` and POSTs with exponential-backoff retry; OpenTelemetry tracing per batch |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/audit/buffered/buffered.go` | **Buffered/batching backend**: wraps any delegate with an in-memory channel queue; collects up to `MaxBatchSize` events or `MaxBatchWait` time, then flushes; optional token-bucket throttling (`ThrottleQPS`/`ThrottleBurst`); async vs. synchronous delegate; used by both log (blocking/batch modes) and webhook (batch mode) |
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/audit.go` | **Server-level wiring**: `AuditOptions`, `AuditLogOptions`, `AuditWebhookOptions`, `AuditBatchOptions`, `AuditTruncateOptions`; `ApplyTo` builds the policy evaluator, constructs log and webhook backends (each wrapped in buffered then truncate layers), joins them with `appendBackend`/`Union` into `c.AuditBackend`; `ignoreErrorsBackend` (ModeBlocking); default configs for log vs. webhook batch behaviour |

---

## 4. Audit Truncation for Large Request/Response Bodies

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/audit/truncate/truncate.go` | **Truncate backend**: `Config` (`MaxEventSize` default 100 KB, `MaxBatchSize` default 10 MB); `ProcessEvents` measures each event's serialised size via `calcSize` (using a `sizer` io.Writer); if `size > MaxEventSize` and `Level >= Request`, `truncate()` strips `RequestObject` and `ResponseObject` and sets annotation `audit.k8s.io/truncated=true`; if still too large, event is dropped with an error; batches are split when cumulative size exceeds `MaxBatchSize` |

---
